raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, my Citi credit card ( old lastXXXX XXXX ) had an unauthorized charge from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  shown online ) for {$490.00} ( I have never bought anything from this merchant ). I reported the fraud immediately, and Citi issued a replacement card on XX/XX/XXXX ( new XXXX XXXX ). 

Citi initially posted a credit on XX/XX/XXXX labeled XXXX XXXX ITEM UNDER INVESTIGATION for - {$490.00}, and then the merchant posted a second credit on XX/XX/XXXX for - {$490.00}. In early XX/XX/XXXX, both credits were reversed/removed, and the account again shows I owe approximately the original unauthorized amount ( ~ {$490.00} ). Citis customer-facing online system only partially reflects what occurred, but the key fact is that the net balance remains owed for the fraudulent charge. 

I have made repeated attempts to resolve this by phone, spending multiple hours and being transferred among Customer Service, Fraud, and Account Specialists : XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX hour, transferred across 3 departments. 

XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, transferred across XXXX departments. I was explicitly told the balance would be corrected to {$0.00} by XX/XX/XXXX at the latest. 

XX/XX/XXXX : another call with multiple transfers ; I was told there is no timeframe for the account to be corrected. 

Citi representatives repeatedly state I was already refunded, but those credits were later removed, and my balance still reflects the original unauthorized amount, meaning I have not actually been made whole.
